pattern: A two-pointer partition swaps an out-of-place odd at the front with an out-of-place even at the back, grouping evens before odds in a single linear pass without extra storage.
hints: You only need every even value to sit somewhere before every odd value; the relative order within each group is free.
Scan from both ends: a front pointer looking for a misplaced odd and a back pointer looking for a misplaced even.
When the front finds an odd and the back finds an even, swap them and step both inward; stop when the pointers meet.

edge_inputs.description: small mix of evens and odds
all odd values, nothing moves to the front
all even values stay in front

stress.description: random arrays mixing evens and odds across many lengths
stress.note: validator checks multiset equality plus the evens-before-odds invariant
